title: Using Crypto to Trade Gold & US Markets (Full Walkthrough)
author: ECKrown
summary_bullets: In this video, I want to show you how you can use your cryptos like your tethers, your USDC, your bitcoins, your salon, your ethereums, your uh camel coins, whatever to to put trades on other markets.
When I say other markets, I'm talking about gold, silver, platinum, platium, all of the major US indices like SPY, NASDAQ, Dow Jones, and even other world markets as well, plus many, many more.
all of the major US equities as well and even several different Forex pairs.
That is going to be through Bybit, which is the link in the description below, which will give you several bonuses.
But of course, full disclosure, it is an affiliate link, but I want to walk you through how you actually fund your account, how you put on trades, these sort of things.
Honestly, if you're familiar with Bybit or any of the major crypto exchanges, you're already going to know how this works.
But it is one of those times where I think it's very important to kind of focus on this because I know a lot of people don't have access to the US markets.
because I have direct experience with it, not particularly through through myself, but through my wife Elsa, who is not a US citizen.
She was trying to sign up to a Vanguard account to get uh, you know, access to the US markets when we were living in Europe.
And there were so many [ __ ] hoops to jump through in order to get this damn account.

Honestly, if you're familiar with Bybit or any of the major crypto exchanges, you're already going to know how this works.
She was trying to sign up to a Vanguard account to get uh, you know, access to the US markets when we were living in Europe.
Uh but yeah if you're not US person it's probably the easiest way to get exposure to these major markets which you know are the ones that are typically moving.
But of course, if you're just looking at Bitcoin, there's been [ __ ] all going on for the past month and for the couple months before that, you know, pretty much nothing going on over here as well.
So, if you are a career trader or a career investor, you know, the opportunity cost is a real thing.
But if you do want those bonuses, then yes, there is a nice benefit with that.
I don't really know why you'd want to do that, but hey, if you do, it's all there for you.
And for the major indices, you got all the major world ones like the Hang Sang index, the European 50, which I guess if you want access or exposure to regulation, that's the one to do it with.
Um, but if you actually did have an account and you weren't limited by your geographical location, then you'll have a green and a red button respectively right here.
And if you want to buy, you hit the green button.
If you want to sell, you hit the red button.
You know if you want to have like a trigger price that then initiates another order you do that through this.
Like if you want to buy a pullback after a breakout for example, you do something like that.
Of course, also on your order entry screen, if you can actually go through this, then you can also set up your takerit and your stop loss in advance.
But at the very least, you can protect yourself and and manage risk that way.
And honestly, if you want to be a real trader, if you want to be a real investor, you do need to be able to access other markets.

And of course when Bitcoin is doing you know very little over here as you can see for the past month or so now uh it is massively advantageous to take your money and go to a different market and find the opportunity there.

Now like I said there is a very very nice um uh bonus on buyer right now if you specifically use the link in the description below um that we go through all sort of different promotions and I usually always go for fee discounts but I know a lot of people aren't the biggest fans of fee discounts.
Maybe if you're not, you know, not so much of an active trader, totally get that.
